title: Support procedural concurrent assertions
body:
Support procedural concurrent assertions, or at least the limited case below, which is rewritten from UVM tests/20sequences/90Mantis/3633_bad_sequence_macros, tests/35objections/03basic/06xbus, tests/80examples/10ubus.

Supporting this requires a ugly queue, per IEEE 16.14.6.  Given the UVM usage doesn't need this (and perhaps is even a test bug that it uses `assert property` instead of `assert`), we may want to only support this limited form and evaluate immediately.

Example below currently gives:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_0.v:16:5: Unsupported: Procedural concurrent assertion with clocking event inside always (IEEE 1800-2023 16.14.6)
   16 |     assert property (disable iff (!rst_l) ($onehot(grant) |-> !$isunknown(addr)))
      |     ^~~~~~
```

```
module t (
    input clk,
    input rst_l,
    input [2:0] grant,
    input [7:0] addr
);

  always @(negedge clk) begin
    assertUnknown :
    assert property (disable iff (!rst_l) ($onehot(grant) |-> !$isunknown(addr)))
    else $error("X or Z");
  end

  initial begin
    $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
    $finish;
  end

endmodule
```
code_blocks:
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t/t_0.v:16:5: Unsupported: Procedural concurrent assertion with clocking event inside always (IEEE 1800-2023 16.14.6)
   16 |     assert property (disable iff (!rst_l) ($onehot(grant) |-> !$isunknown(addr)))
      |     ^~~~~~
module t (
    input clk,
    input rst_l,
    input [2:0] grant,
    input [7:0] addr
);

  always @(negedge clk) begin
    assertUnknown :
    assert property (disable iff (!rst_l) ($onehot(grant) |-> !$isunknown(addr)))
    else $error("X or Z");
  end

  initial begin
    $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
    $finish;
  end

endmodule
